title: Broken Access Control
description: Learn how improper access control in web applications allows attackers to access restricted pages, data, or functions they are not authorized to use.

attack_stages.title: User Role Assumption
attack_stages.description: The application assigns different roles such as user, admin, or moderator.

attack_stages.title: Weak Authorization Checks
attack_stages.description: The website fails to properly verify user permissions on every request.

attack_stages.title: Unauthorized Access Attempt
attack_stages.description: The attacker tries to access restricted pages or functions.

attack_stages.title: Access Control Bypass
attack_stages.description: The attacker successfully bypasses authorization controls.

attack_stages.title: Unauthorized Data Access
attack_stages.description: Sensitive information or restricted functionality becomes accessible.

prevention_points.title: Enforce Authorization on Every Request
prevention_points.description: Ensure that every page, API call, and action checks user permissions.

prevention_points.title: Least Privilege Principle
prevention_points.description: Users should only have access to what they absolutely need.

prevention_points.title: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
prevention_points.description: Implement strict role definitions and enforce them consistently.

prevention_points.title: Regular Security Testing
prevention_points.description: Continuously test access control for different user roles to detect flaws early.

nodes.title: Scenario
nodes.text: A web application allows users to log in and access different pages depending on their role, such as regular users and administrators.

nodes.title: What is Access Control?
nodes.text: Access control determines what authenticated users are allowed to see or do within a web application. It ensures that users can only access resources that match their assigned permissions.

nodes.title: Broken Access Control
nodes.text: Broken access control occurs when a website fails to properly enforce authorization rules, allowing users to access pages or perform actions they should not be permitted to.

nodes.title: Attacker Goal
nodes.text: The attacker wants to access restricted pages or administrative functions that are normally unavailable to regular users.

nodes.title: How might an attacker try to exploit broken access control?

choices.text: Attempt to access restricted pages or functions directly
Encrypt the database from the browser

nodes.title: Try Again
nodes.text: Attackers do not modify system security directly. They attempt to access restricted resources that are not properly protected.

nodes.title: Access Control Bypass
nodes.text: Due to missing or weak authorization checks, the attacker successfully accesses restricted functionality or pages intended only for privileged users.

nodes.title: Impact
nodes.text: Broken access control can lead to exposure of sensitive data, unauthorized modifications, and misuse of administrative features.

nodes.title: How to Prevent This
nodes.text: Organizations should enforce authorization checks on every request, apply the principle of least privilege, implement role-based access control (RBAC), and regularly test permissions across different user roles.

nodes.title: Attack Replay
nodes.text: Review how missing authorization checks allowed an attacker to bypass restrictions and access sensitive functionality.

nodes.title: Scenario Complete
nodes.text: Broken access control is one of the most critical web vulnerabilities, allowing attackers to bypass restrictions and access unauthorized resources if proper authorization checks are not enforced.
